title: GASCADE Begins Preparatory Work for HYLU Hydrogen Pipeline to Ludwigshafen
content: GASCADE Gastransport GmbH has initiated preparatory work for the HYLU hydrogen pipeline, a 15-kilometer link connecting Lampertheim to Ludwigshafen am Rhein. Part of the "Flow â€“ making hydrogen happen" program and the German hydrogen core grid, the pipeline will transport hydrogen converted from the MIDAL gas transmission line to decarbonize local industry in Hesse, Baden-WÃ¼rttemberg, and Rhineland-Palatinate. Scheduled for completion in 2029, the project also involves building a hydrogen pressure regulating and measuring station (WDRM). Additionally, a new gas pressure regulating station (GDRM) is planned for Lampertheim by 2028 to maintain the region's natural gas supply. Mapping, surveying, building site investigations, and explosive ordnance surveys will run from March to fall 2026 to prepare for the planning approval process. The infrastructure aims to secure the region's competitiveness in the chemical, power, and manufacturing sectors.
author: Analysts Account
